title: HCSS Rizzoma Business Topic
content:
HCSS Rizzoma Business Topic

#MetaTopic

  • Oneliner (updated)

    • This is the 'landing page' that opens up when you get into Rizzoma if you're a paying Rizzoma business account member. We want to keep 'bare bones' for just a small group of people — to minimize the costs. It will not be updated.

    • Unless we see a reason for it.

    • — the 'real' Metatopic is here.

  • Relevant links

    • New to Rizzoma

  • What is Rizzoma (revised)

    • Rizzoma is fully 'democratic' (Russia-based) real-time online collaboration tool that we feel is great for the type of knowledge work we do: feel free to jump in anywhere and either modify/augment/critique/… what is there.

  • First steps in Rizzoma

    1. Click the 'plus' sign at the end of this line and watch the 1:17' introductory video

    2. Make sure you read the one-liner, the research assignment of the project you have been assigned to and skim at least the main structure of the 'research design' section (and you may want to drill down into the sections that you will be working on)

    3. The most useful button on Rizzoma is the green button on the top right of your pane here that says 'next'. That will light up in green whenever somebody posts sthg new in whatever topic your cursor is in. If you click on the button, you'll jump to the first blip you haven't read yet

    4. If you got invited to a big topic in which all blips are 'green', you may want to mark the entire topic as 'read', and then, from the top, start using the green button to just see the 'new' things that are added

Managing the green: How to stay up to date on a big Rizzoma topic

  • Golden rules - these are critically important to a productive use of this tool, so please do read them

  • Navigation/Search hints in Rizzoma

  • Confidential information

  • Rizzoma and HCSS

title: HCSS Rizzoma Business Topic
content:
HCSS Rizzoma Business Topic

#MetaTopic

  • Oneliner

  • Relevant links

    • New to Rizzoma

  • What is Rizzoma

  • First steps in Rizzoma

    1. Click the 'plus' sign at the end of this line and watch the 1:17' introductory video

    2. Make sure you read the one-liner, the research assignment of the project you have been assigned to and skim at least the main structure of the 'research design' section (and you may want to drill down into the sections that you will be working on)
